title: To Kill a Mockingbird
description: A novel about racial injustice in the Deep South seen through the eyes of a child.
author: Harper Lee

description: Dystopian novel exploring totalitarianism, surveillance, and individualism.
author: George Orwell

title: Pride and Prejudice
description: Romantic novel that also critiques the British landed gentry at the end of the 18th century.
author: Jane Austen

title: The Great Gatsby
description: A tragic story of wealth, longing, and the American Dream in the Jazz Age.
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

title: The Catcher in the Rye
description: A teenager's experiences in New York City after being expelled from prep school.
author: J.D. Salinger

title: The Hobbit
description: A fantasy adventure following Bilbo Baggins on his quest with dwarves to reclaim treasure.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
description: The first book in the Harry Potter series introducing Harry and Hogwarts.
author: J.K. Rowling

title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
description: First part of the epic fantasy trilogy about the quest to destroy the One Ring.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

title: Moby-Dick
description: An obsessive quest of Captain Ahab to chase the white whale, Moby Dick.
author: Herman Melville

title: Jane Eyre
description: A strong-willed woman's journey through hardship to independence and love.
author: Charlotte Brontë

title: The Da Vinci Code
description: A symbologist and a cryptologist uncover a religious mystery protected by a secret society.
author: Dan Brown

title: The Alchemist
description: A shepherd's journey to find treasure teaches him about listening to his heart.
author: Paulo Coelho

title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
description: A mystery thriller about a journalist and a hacker investigating a decades-old disappearance.
author: Stieg Larsson

title: The Hunger Games
description: A dystopian contest forces children to fight to the death as spectacle and control.
author: Suzanne Collins

title: Gone Girl
description: A thriller about a marriage gone wrong and the media circus around a disappearance.
author: Gillian Flynn

title: The Kite Runner
description: A story of friendship and redemption set against the backdrop of Afghanistan's turbulent history.
author: Khaled Hosseini

title: The Handmaid's Tale
description: A dystopian tale where women are subjugated and valued only for reproduction.
author: Margaret Atwood

title: Brave New World
description: A futuristic society driven by technology, conditioning, and consumerism.
author: Aldous Huxley

title: The Chronicles of Narnia: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
description: Four children enter a magical land and join the fight against a white witch.
author: C.S. Lewis

title: The Fault in Our Stars
description: A touching young adult novel about love and mortality.
author: John Green

title: The Road
description: A post-apocalyptic journey of a father and son struggling to survive.
author: Cormac McCarthy

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
description: A narrative of humanity's creation and evolution exploring history, science, and culture.
author: Yuval Noah Harari

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
description: A psychologist explores the two systems that drive the way we think.
author: Daniel Kahneman

title: The Odyssey
description: Homer's epic of Odysseus's long journey home after the Trojan War.
author: Homer

title: A Game of Thrones
description: Noble families vie for control of the Iron Throne in a fantasy realm.
author: George R.R. Martin

title: The Girl on the Train
description: An unreliable narrator becomes entangled in a missing person investigation.
author: Paula Hawkins

title: The Shining
description: A family's winter caretaking of an isolated hotel leads to supernatural terror.
author: Stephen King

title: Dracula
description: A Gothic horror novel about the vampire Count Dracula's attempt to move to England.
author: Bram Stoker

title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
description: A young man remains eternally youthful while his portrait ages and records his sins.
author: Oscar Wilde

title: The Count of Monte Cristo
description: A tale of betrayal, revenge, and redemption set in 19th-century France.
author: Alexandre Dumas

title: Les Misérables
description: An epic story of justice, love, and revolution in 19th-century France.
author: Victor Hugo

title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
description: A comedic science fiction tale of Arthur Dent's travels through space.
author: Douglas Adams

title: Fahrenheit 451
description: A dystopia where books are outlawed and 'firemen' burn any that are found.
author: Ray Bradbury

title: Meditations
description: Personal writings by Marcus Aurelius on Stoic philosophy and practice.
author: Marcus Aurelius

title: The Secret
description: A self-help book promoting the law of attraction and positive thinking.
author: Rhonda Byrne

title: Becoming
description: Michelle Obama's memoir about her life, career, and time as First Lady.
author: Michelle Obama

title: Atomic Habits
description: A practical guide to building good habits and breaking bad ones.
author: James Clear

title: The Silent Patient
description: A psychotherapist tries to uncover why a patient stopped speaking after a violent act.
author: Alex Michaelides

title: The Power of Now
description: A guide to spiritual enlightenment through living fully in the present moment.
author: Eckhart Tolle

title: Outliers
description: An examination of the factors that contribute to high levels of success.
author: Malcolm Gladwell

title: The Book Thief
description: A young girl's relationship with books in Nazi Germany narrated by Death.
author: Markus Zusak

title: A Brief History of Time
description: Stephen Hawking's exploration of cosmology, black holes, and the universe.
author: Stephen Hawking

title: The Silence of the Lambs
description: An FBI trainee consults an incarcerated cannibalistic serial killer to catch another killer.
author: Thomas Harris

title: The Outsiders
description: A coming-of-age novel about class conflict and brotherhood.
author: S.E. Hinton

title: Wolf Hall
description: A historical novel about Thomas Cromwell's rise to power in the court of Henry VIII.
author: Hilary Mantel

title: The Name of the Rose
description: A medieval mystery involving a friar investigating a series of murders in an abbey.
author: Umberto Eco

title: The Little Prince
description: A poetic tale of loneliness, friendship, love, and loss told through a child's eyes.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry

title: The Secret Garden
description: A story of healing and friendship centered on a hidden, neglected garden.
author: Frances Hodgson Burnett
